title: Level 449
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 449: "It's Snowing Like It's the End of the World"'''}} {{Background|file=Level 449.jpg}} {{New Theme}} __NOTOC__ {{Notice|body=Red links will be filled soon.}}

[[File:Snowy bushes.jpg|400px|center|link=]]

Hello, my love. It's been a long time, hasn't it? I'm still here, and somehow I can still remember you. Maybe it's because the only thing I've held onto all this time is our memories. I don't know how much time has passed; I don't even know who I am anymore. But I'm sure you're still there, my love, waiting for me as always... Even if I don't know your worth. I'm being very emotional, aren't I? Ah, you used to say I didn't understand poetry, remember, my love? This place makes you emotional even if you don't want it to. There is a deep sadness inside me, as if there is a huge void in the middle of my chest that can never be filled. The cold burns through it. It makes me feel helpless. Do you know that I always remember you at times like this? Ah, my love, if only you could know... ==
Description
 == Level 449 is a town with constant snowfall. The town is notable for its buildings of varying sizes, sparse trees, and snow-covered parks. The buildings have an old Scandinavian architectural style, featuring many windows and no decorative patterns. They are typically light in color, though heavily weathered. The buildings appear to be clustered within a defined area; despite the spacing between them, it has been confirmed that there are more than eighty structures present. Additionally, it was determined that most of the food resources at the level were found in vending machines and boxes inside these buildings. Several kilometers away from the main cluster of buildings, it is possible to encounter scattered children’s playgrounds separated by wide, empty stretches of land. These playgrounds are generally damaged and deteriorated, yet empty food packaging can often be found among the toys. Despite this, no viable food sources have been identified in any outdoor area of the level. Snowfall in the level is not particularly heavy, and the snow depth is known to remain constant at approximately 23 centimeters. The air temperature typically ranges between –16°C and –20°C, creating a dry, biting cold rather than blizzard conditions. Prolonged exposure to this environment can cause health problems for travelers; low humidity and constant wind allow the cold to penetrate clothing and skin over time. After spending time at this level, body temperature decreases significantly enough to impair movement and coordination, whether thick or thin clothing is worn. Skin contact with exposed metal surfaces and any frozen areas can cause pain and increase the risk of cold-related injuries. Moreover, particles present in the level's atmosphere can cause serious damage to human lungs. After spending the first 30 seconds at the level, the traveler will feel as if the air in their lungs has run out, this sensation is often described as "chest tightness." This chest tightness causes a sudden increase in the person's stress level and anxiety. In this context, the traveler's thoughts turn to blame, questioning, and judgment. They may yell and scream out of nowhere, even hurting themselves. But after a certain period, they begin to neutralize. Travelers who have been at this level before describe this feeling as “emotional numbness". Initially, the human mind begins to numb as it spends time in the level's atmosphere. An intense feeling of nostalgia descends upon the person, and their movements often become aimless. Thus, the intense anger within them subsides, and they feel the flow of old memories. During this process, the aimlessness of movements makes it difficult for the person to find a source of nourishment and a warm place at that level.
[[File:Second pic.jpg|400px|center|link=]]

Sometimes, as I'm walking around, I feel like I see you. At those times, such hope fills me that even if I knew I was going to die, I would run towards you. But I know, deep down, that I'm here alone and that I left you. But you know I didn't do this on purpose, right? I don't know how long I've been wandering around here, but I never wanted to leave you there, my love, please believe me. ==
Entities
 == Level 449 has confirmed to have Hounds in the back area of Buildings. Due to the high number of these entities, travelers who interact with them usually do not survive. The entity with the highest count at this level is [[Thorns]]. This entity is found in snowflakes and as particles in the air. When it enters the respiratory system, it activates and begins to grow inside the lungs, taking the shape of a thorn. By that, it obstructs breathing and rapidly leads infected individuals to death. If the wanderer cannot find any enclosed space upon reaching the altitude, their lungs will become infected, and they will die from internal hemorrhage. However, the chances of survival can be increased through certain precautions, such as wearing a mask.
[[File:Level 449.3.jpg|400px|center|link=]]

I sometimes wonder if you forgot me. Do you remember me, my darling? But you wouldn't forget, no, oh my beautiful darling, you could never forget us. You always kept the photos from the first day we met, the flowers I gave you—we used to look at them together every night. Do you see, my love, I remember, you can't be angry with me now, can you? ==
Colonies & Outposts
 == Due to the nature and the isolation of the level, it is not possible to form long-lasting groups or colonies. Travelers on this level may occasionally encounter other travelers, but they lose sight of each other after a very short time.
[[File:Заринск Зимнее утро .jpg|400px|center|link=]]

I'm starting to lose feeling in my hands, darling. It hurts to breathe, like my lungs are bleeding. Maybe I'm dying, what do you think? If you were here, you'd get angry at me for talking like this and wrap your knitted scarf around me. That would be nice, y'know? I always loved the knitted stuff we had in our home. Even the ones you knitted for my cats. I met with some people here, and they advised me to stop thinking about a way out. Maybe they were right, perhaps I am stuck here forever... ==
Entrances & Exits
 == ===
Entrances
 === *At [[Level 324]], a sudden snowfall may bring you here after a certain period of time. *Spending time on a snowy hill at [[Level 822]] can lead you here. ===
Exits
 === *No-clipping inside of the buildings can lead one to [[Level 76]]. *Wandering outside for a long time can lead one to [[Level 93]]'s outdoor area. It's snowing harshly, snowing like it's the end of the world, or whatever dimension I am in. Goodbye my love, forgive me for everything I couldn't be.
